backlog.notes: The project pipeline records include directories, reports missing include directories, reports include directories that resolve outside the repository, applies CLI include-directory overrides, and passes resolved paths to source frontends that expose include-path preprocessor options. Include semantics remain backend-dependent, so this remains partial project support.

The project pipeline records include directories, reports missing include directories, reports include directories that resolve outside the repository, applies CLI include-directory overrides, and passes resolved paths to source frontends that expose include-path preprocessor options. Include semantics remain backend-dependent, so this remains partial project support.

The project pipeline records include directories, reports missing include directories, reports include directories that resolve outside the repository, applies CLI include-directory overrides, and passes resolved paths to source frontends that expose include-path preprocessor options. Include semantics remain backend-dependent, so this remains partial project support.

The project pipeline records define sets and named variant metadata in portability reports, preserves define and variant maps alongside counts, validates malformed define/variant metadata and undeclared artifact variants in reports, rejects malformed define and variant TOML values before scan, applies CLI define overrides, passes defines to source frontends that expose preprocessor options, and expands configured named variants into separate artifact attempts with base defines merged with variant overrides. Native preprocessor semantics remain backend-dependent, so this remains partial project support.

The project pipeline records define sets and named variant metadata in portability reports, preserves define and variant maps alongside counts, validates malformed define/variant metadata and undeclared artifact variants in reports, rejects malformed define and variant TOML values before scan, applies CLI define overrides, passes defines to source frontends that expose preprocessor options, and expands configured named variants into separate artifact attempts with base defines merged with variant overrides. Native preprocessor semantics remain backend-dependent, so this remains partial project support.

The project pipeline records define sets and named variant metadata in portability reports, preserves define and variant maps alongside counts, validates malformed define/variant metadata and undeclared artifact variants in reports, rejects malformed define and variant TOML values before scan, applies CLI define overrides, passes defines to source frontends that expose preprocessor options, and expands configured named variants into separate artifact attempts with base defines merged with variant overrides. Native preprocessor semantics remain backend-dependent, so this remains partial project support.

The project pipeline batches through the existing single-file translator, normalizes duplicate explicit targets, applies source backend overrides, records unsupported target diagnostics, preserves relative output paths, refuses to write artifacts outside the repository, and records failures per artifact. Full multi-corpus target coverage remains ongoing.

The project pipeline batches through the existing single-file translator, normalizes duplicate explicit targets, applies source backend overrides, records unsupported target diagnostics, preserves relative output paths, refuses to write artifacts outside the repository, and records failures per artifact. Full multi-corpus target coverage remains ongoing.

The project pipeline batches through the existing single-file translator, normalizes duplicate explicit targets, applies source backend overrides, records unsupported target diagnostics, preserves relative output paths, refuses to write artifacts outside the repository, and records failures per artifact. Full multi-corpus target coverage remains ongoing.

Project reports include configured source override maps, source hashes, relative paths, source backend names, intermediate CrossGL provenance, and file-granularity artifact source-map anchors. Report validation accepts generated source maps, records per-artifact source hash status and aggregate hash-status counts, and rejects malformed project source override metadata, source-map records, inconsistent source-map anchors, source hash records, source artifacts whose current file hash does not match the report, missing source artifacts with recorded hashes, and provenance records before artifact validation. Fine-grained source maps are not implemented in the translator project pipeline yet.

Project reports include configured source override maps, source hashes, relative paths, source backend names, intermediate CrossGL provenance, and file-granularity artifact source-map anchors. Report validation accepts generated source maps, records per-artifact source hash status and aggregate hash-status counts, and rejects malformed project source override metadata, source-map records, inconsistent source-map anchors, source hash records, source artifacts whose current file hash does not match the report, missing source artifacts with recorded hashes, and provenance records before artifact validation. Fine-grained source maps are not implemented in the translator project pipeline yet.

Project reports include configured source override maps, source hashes, relative paths, source backend names, intermediate CrossGL provenance, and file-granularity artifact source-map anchors. Report validation accepts generated source maps, records per-artifact source hash status and aggregate hash-status counts, and rejects malformed project source override metadata, source-map records, inconsistent source-map anchors, source hash records, source artifacts whose current file hash does not match the report, missing source artifacts with recorded hashes, and provenance records before artifact validation. Fine-grained source maps are not implemented in the translator project pipeline yet.

Project reports can attach a repository-relative external corpus manifest, skip malformed manifest entries with structured diagnostics, and summarize declared valid paths, present and missing entries, discovered translation units, source-backend, target, and artifact rollups for manifest-backed project runs, and text inspection surfaces the same rollups. The pinned reduced-corpus manifest includes a focused DirectX reduction covered by backend fixture tests. This is a coverage and accounting layer only: CrossTL does not clone upstream repositories, run native build systems, or claim whole-corpus semantic parity from reduced backend fixtures.

The pinned reduced-corpus manifest includes a focused OpenGL reduction covered by backend fixture tests. This is a coverage and accounting layer only: CrossTL does not clone upstream repositories, run native build systems, or claim whole-corpus semantic parity from reduced backend fixtures.

The pinned reduced-corpus manifest includes a focused Metal reduction covered by backend fixture tests. This is a coverage and accounting layer only: CrossTL does not clone upstream repositories, run native build systems, or claim whole-corpus semantic parity from reduced backend fixtures.

features.description: Emit target-language source or target representation from CrossGL.
features.name: CrossGL to target code generation

features.description: Parse backend source language and regenerate CrossGL.
features.name: Native source to CrossGL

features.description: Tokenize backend source language constructs used by the native parser.
features.name: Native lexer coverage

features.description: Handle native preprocessor directives, includes, defines, and conditional compilation where the backend language exposes them.
features.name: Native preprocessor handling

features.description: CrossGL vertex entry point lowering.
features.name: Vertex stage

features.description: CrossGL fragment entry point lowering.
features.name: Fragment/pixel stage

features.description: CrossGL compute entry point lowering.
features.name: Compute stage

features.description: Geometry shader stage and primitive stream shape validation.
features.name: Geometry stage

metal.notes: Metal has no traditional native geometry shader stage, so CrossGL geometry entry points lower to deterministic Metal helper functions with CrossGL geometry-stage metadata and compile-oriented PointStream/LineStream/TriangleStream placeholders. The lowering preserves geometry input primitive qualifiers and output stream element types in metadata, emits stream Append/RestartStrip calls against placeholder stream helpers, requires positive maxvertexcount attributes, and validates point/line/triangle/adjacent input primitive array arity.

features.description: Tessellation control/hull and tessellation evaluation/domain stages.
features.name: Tessellation stages

metal.notes: Metal tessellation control and evaluation stages lower to deterministic helper functions with CrossGL tessellation metadata and compile-oriented InputPatch/OutputPatch placeholders. The lowering preserves domain, partitioning, output topology, outputcontrolpoints, patchconstantfunc, maxtessfactor, patch parameter element/count metadata, and tessellation helper semantics while validating patch signatures and domain-location shapes.

opengl.notes: GLSL tessellation control/evaluation stages lower native layout metadata and tessellation builtins, while unsupported patchconstantfunc metadata is rejected with a deterministic diagnostic instead of emitting invalid GLSL.

features.description: Modern mesh-style stage entry points.
features.name: Mesh/task/amplification stages

directx.notes: Entry points lower to HLSL mesh/amplification shader attributes with deterministic stage names, numthreads from local-size layouts or @numthreads, mesh output topology attributes, mesh output signatures for vertices/indices/primitives, mesh payload parameters, groupshared DispatchMesh payload forwarding, and native SetMeshOutputCounts/DispatchMesh calls. The backend validates output roles/counts/topology, system-value parameter types, SetMeshOutputCounts arity/bounds/stage/control-flow/write ordering, DispatchMesh arity/group-count bounds/stage/control-flow, payload address space, payload type matching, and required payload dispatches.

metal.notes: Entry points lower to valid Metal object/mesh function attributes with deterministic names, max_total_threads_per_threadgroup from local-size layouts or explicit attributes, generated or explicit mesh output signatures/topology, SetMeshOutputCounts primitive counts, whole vertex/index/primitive output writes, single-member output member writes, multi-member partial output writes through accumulators, object/mesh payload parameters, DirectX-style @mesh_payload parameters, and DispatchMesh payload argument forwarding through object_data payloads. The backend validates literal output-count bounds, write ordering, helper ordering, if/switch branch dominance, loop-exit dominance for post-branch/output writes, payload address spaces, dispatch payload source/type shapes, grid arguments, and generated name collisions.

opengl.notes: Entry points and combined-stage names lower with GL_EXT_mesh_shader enablement, local-size layouts, mesh output topology/count layouts, taskPayloadSharedEXT payload storage, DispatchMesh payload assignment, native EmitMeshTasksEXT/SetMeshOutputsEXT calls, mesh output signatures for vertices/indices/primitives, helper forwarding through SetVertex/SetPrimitive, and topology-specific primitive index builtins. The backend validates layout aliases/conflicts, positive count constants, DispatchMesh arity/count/payload shapes, mesh output parameter shapes/counts, SetMeshOutputCounts arity/count bounds, output literal/dynamic indices, whole-output assignment shapes, helper arity/value types, and expression-context misuse.

features.description: Ray generation, hit, miss, callable, and intersection stages.
features.name: Ray tracing stages

directx.notes: Ray generation, closest-hit, any-hit, miss, callable, and intersection stages lower to HLSL library shader attributes with deterministic stage names. Payload, callable-data, and hit-attribute stage parameters map to native HLSL inout/in signatures with role, order, and type validation. RaytracingAccelerationStructure resources and RayDesc operands preserve HLSL ray-tracing types and bindings, TraceRay, CallShader, ReportHit, IgnoreHit, and AcceptHitAndEndSearch lower to native HLSL calls with stage, arity, operand, ray-flag, and instance-mask validation, and RayQuery methods lower to native HLSL methods with receiver/argument validation and result type inference.

metal.notes: Ray generation payloads lower to device references; acceleration structures and intersection_function_table resources lower to Metal raytracing buffer parameters; full TraceRay calls lower to ray plus intersector intersection queries with xcrun metal3 coverage for helper-threaded calls, instance masks, primitive_acceleration_structure shape, single compatible intersection_function_table dispatch, and thread-local payload forwarding. Unsupported TraceRay payload forms, such as missing compatible tables or non-thread payload parameters, emit compile-safe diagnostics. RayQuery declarations and methods lower to compile-safe CglRayQuery placeholder helpers because Metal has no HLSL-style RayQuery object. CallShader lowers to visible_function_table dispatch for explicit tables or a single global table; visible_function_table and intersection_function_table ray-generation parameters lower with compiler-validated buffer bindings; hit, miss, any-hit, and callable stages lower to visible functions with ray_data payload/callable-data references; intersection stages lower to Metal intersection attributes for triangle and bounding-box functions. Metal-prohibited arrayed visible_function_table and intersection_function_table globals or parameters emit compile-safe diagnostics while scalar table globals, parameters, explicit callable dispatch, and intersection-table TraceRay paths remain compiler validated.

opengl.notes: Ray generation, closest-hit, any-hit, miss, intersection, and callable stages lower with deterministic combined-stage entry names or target-stage main entry points and GL_EXT_ray_tracing enablement. Acceleration-structure resources, shaderRecordEXT buffers, rayPayloadEXT/rayPayloadInEXT, hitAttributeEXT, callableDataEXT/callableDataInEXT, TraceRay, CallShader, IgnoreHit, ReportHit, and ray hit position-fetch extension scoping are covered by GLSL codegen tests.

features.description: Input parameter semantics and target builtin attributes.
features.name: Stage parameter semantics

features.description: Function return semantics, wrapper output structs, and invalid-return validation.
features.name: Direct function return semantics

features.description: Input/output struct member semantics and builtin type validation.
features.name: Struct member semantics

features.description: Resource binding/register/location assignment and collision avoidance.
features.name: Explicit and automatic resource bindings

features.description: CrossGL cbuffer/uniform block lowering.
features.name: Constant/uniform buffers

features.description: StructuredBuffer, RWStructuredBuffer, SSBO-style storage, and access helpers.
features.name: Structured/storage buffers

directx.notes: Covers StructuredBuffer/RWStructuredBuffer declarations, fixed and inferred resource arrays, load/store/dimensions helpers, ByteAddressBuffer scalar/vector load/store helpers, byte-address vector helper resource/value shape diagnostics, native HLSL ByteAddressBuffer/RWByteAddressBuffer vector method import, register-space allocation, type aliases, append/consume buffers, access-mode diagnostics for readonly/append/consume misuse, rasterizer-ordered typed/structured buffers, and GLSL SSBO single-array round-trips.

metal.notes: Covers StructuredBuffer/RWStructuredBuffer as device pointers, fixed and inferred resource arrays, load/store helpers, buffer_dimensions through generated sidecar length buffers, and AppendStructuredBuffer/ConsumeStructuredBuffer through generated sidecar atomic counter buffers.

opengl.notes: Covers StructuredBuffer/RWStructuredBuffer as SSBOs, fixed and inferred resource arrays, load/store/dimensions helpers, and AppendStructuredBuffer/ConsumeStructuredBuffer lowering through generated sidecar counter SSBOs.

features.description: Fixed and unsized texture, sampler, image, and buffer arrays.
features.name: Resource arrays

directx.notes: Covers fixed, expression-sized, and inferred unsized texture/sampler arrays, shadow arrays, formatted image arrays, multisample image arrays, and StructuredBuffer/ByteAddressBuffer resource arrays with deterministic size-conflict diagnostics.

metal.notes: Covers fixed, expression-sized, and inferred unsized texture/sampler arrays, shadow arrays, formatted image arrays, multisample image arrays, and Metal buffer resource arrays with deterministic size-conflict diagnostics.

opengl.notes: Covers fixed, expression-sized, and inferred unsized texture arrays, shadow arrays, formatted image arrays, multisample image arrays, and SSBO resource arrays with deterministic size-conflict diagnostics.

features.description: Represent combined and separate texture/sampler models without binding collisions.
features.name: Texture and sampler object model

directx.notes: Covers separate texture and sampler declarations, implicit sampler threading, comparison sampler role separation, arrayed samplers, register-space inheritance, and collision-free binding namespaces.

metal.notes: Covers separate texture and sampler declarations, implicit sampler threading, comparison sampler role separation, arrayed samplers, and collision-free texture/sampler/buffer binding namespaces.

opengl.notes: Covers combined sampler emission, source sampler elision, shadow-compare coordinate construction, sampler/image binding independence, arrayed sampler filtering, and collision-free binding namespaces.

features.description: Lower GLSL-style buffer blocks and preserve deterministic diagnostics for unsupported target shapes.
features.name: GLSL buffer block lowering

directx.notes: Covers std140/std430 fixed-only blocks and mixed metadata plus final runtime-array blocks via ByteAddressBuffer, including bool/int/uint/float scalars, fixed-width scalar alias normalization, vectors, matrices, nested struct leaf fields, struct arrays, layout-distinct aggregate helpers, readonly aggregate helpers, nested aggregate leaf compound stores, aggregate struct load/store with fixed array fields, fixed arrays, int/uint scalar and runtime-array atomics, global-constant fixed sizes, readonly SRV lowering, block arrays, buffer-block parameters, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported layouts/types. Scalar layout, non-final runtime arrays, and non-lowerable member types are intentionally covered by diagnostics under this feature contract.

metal.notes: Covers std140/std430 fixed-only blocks and mixed metadata plus final runtime-array blocks via explicit pointer/offset lowering, including bool/int/uint/float scalars, vectors, matrices, nested struct leaf fields, struct arrays, layout-distinct aggregate helpers, readonly aggregate helpers, nested aggregate leaf compound stores, aggregate struct load/store with fixed array fields, fixed arrays, int/uint scalar and runtime-array atomics, global-constant fixed sizes, readonly const-device lowering, block arrays, fixed-width scalar alias normalization, buffer-block parameter atomics, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported layouts/types. Scalar layout and non-lowerable member types are intentionally covered by diagnostics under this feature contract.

features.description: Translate readonly/writeonly/coherent/volatile/restrict-style resource qualifiers where the target has an equivalent.
features.name: Resource memory qualifiers

directx.notes: coherent/globallycoherent lower to HLSL globallycoherent; readonly/writeonly enforce storage-image access validation; qualifiers without an HLSL resource equivalent do not leak as semantics.

metal.notes: readonly/writeonly lower to Metal texture access modes; qualifiers without a Metal texture equivalent do not leak as attributes.

opengl.notes: GLSL-native resource qualifiers are preserved on globals and helper parameters; globallycoherent lowers to the closest GLSL coherent qualifier.

features.description: Basic sampling with regular and shadow samplers.
features.name: Texture sampling

features.description: Lower explicit LOD, gradient, bias, and offset texture operations.
features.name: Texture LOD, gradient, and offset operations

directx.notes: Covers explicit LOD, gradient, bias, 2D/array offsets, cube/cube-array gradients, 3D offsets, resource arrays, and deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid offset forms.

metal.notes: Covers target-valid explicit LOD, gradient, bias, 2D/array offsets, cube/cube-array gradients, 3D offsets, resource arrays, and deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid offset, 1D LOD/query, and 1D bias/gradient forms.

opengl.notes: Covers explicit LOD, gradient, bias, 2D/array offsets, cube/cube-array gradients, 3D offsets, resource arrays, and deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid offset forms.

features.description: Lower projection-coordinate texture sampling and shadow comparison variants.
features.name: Projected texture operations

directx.notes: Covers planar, array, 1D, cube color, cube-shadow, resource-array, explicit-sampler, and implicit-sampler projected texture and shadow-compare forms. Target-invalid cube-array projection and unsupported offset/role-conflict combinations emit deterministic diagnostics.

metal.notes: Covers planar, array, cube color, cube-shadow, resource-array, explicit-sampler, and implicit/default-sampler projected texture and shadow-compare forms. Target-invalid cube-array projection and unsupported offset forms emit deterministic diagnostics.

opengl.notes: Covers planar, array, cube color, cube-shadow, resource-array, explicit-sampler, and implicit-sampler projected texture and shadow-compare forms. Target-invalid cube-array projection and unsupported LOD/offset forms emit deterministic diagnostics.

features.description: Lower gather, gather-offset, and gather-offsets texture operations.
features.name: Texture gather operations

directx.notes: Covers gather, gather-offset, gather-offsets, dynamic component selection, resource arrays, cube/cube-array gather, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported dimensions and offset forms.

metal.notes: Covers gather, gather-offset, gather-offsets, dynamic component selection, resource arrays, cube/cube-array gather, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported dimensions and offset forms.

opengl.notes: Covers gather, gather-offset, gather-offsets, dynamic component selection, resource arrays, cube/cube-array gather, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported dimensions and offset forms.

features.description: Lower shadow sampler compare operations, including offset and LOD variants where target semantics allow them.
features.name: Shadow compare texture operations

directx.notes: Covers comparison samplers, shadow resource arrays, compare offsets, gather-compare, LOD/gradient compare variants where HLSL permits them, and deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid combinations.

metal.notes: Covers depth textures, shadow resource arrays, compare offsets, gather-compare, LOD/gradient compare variants where Metal permits them, and deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid combinations.

opengl.notes: Covers shadow samplers, shadow resource arrays, compare offsets, gather-compare, LOD/gradient compare variants where GLSL permits them, and deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid combinations.

features.description: Lower size, LOD, levels, and sample-count resource query operations, with deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid query forms.
features.name: Texture query operations

directx.notes: Covers sampler queries, storage-image size queries, multisample level/sample queries, array/cube-array LOD coordinates, and deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid query forms.

metal.notes: Covers sampler queries, storage-image size queries, multisample level/sample queries, array/cube-array LOD coordinates, and deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid query forms.

opengl.notes: Covers sampler queries, storage-image size queries, multisample level/sample queries, array/cube-array LOD coordinates, and deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid query forms.

features.description: Lower integer-coordinate texel fetch and fetch-offset operations, with deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid fetch forms.
features.name: Texel fetch operations

directx.notes: Covers regular 2D/array fetch and offset lowering plus deterministic diagnostics for storage-image, multisample-offset, and cube/cube-array target-invalid fetch forms.

metal.notes: Covers regular 2D/array fetch and offset lowering plus deterministic diagnostics for storage-image, multisample-offset, and cube/cube-array target-invalid fetch forms.

opengl.notes: Covers regular 2D/array fetch and offset lowering plus deterministic diagnostics for storage-image, multisample-offset, and cube/cube-array target-invalid fetch forms.

features.description: Lower multisample texture fetch, query, and comparison forms with deterministic diagnostics for unsupported combinations.
features.name: Multisample texture operations

directx.notes: Covers 2D and 2D-array multisample fetches, sample-count and level queries, resource arrays, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported sample, offset, LOD, compare, and gather combinations.

metal.notes: Covers 2D and 2D-array multisample fetches, sample-count and level queries, resource arrays, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported sample, offset, LOD, compare, and gather combinations.

opengl.notes: Covers 2D and 2D-array multisample fetches, sample-count and level queries, resource arrays, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported sample, offset, LOD, compare, and gather combinations.

features.description: LOD, gradients, offsets, projected coordinates, gathers, compares, and resource queries.
features.name: Advanced texture operations

directx.notes: Covers LOD, gradients, offsets, projected coordinates, gathers, compares, resource queries, multisample query/fetch forms, resource arrays, and deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid advanced combinations.

metal.notes: Covers LOD, gradients, offsets, projected coordinates, gathers, compares, resource queries, multisample query/fetch forms, resource arrays, and deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid advanced combinations.

opengl.notes: Covers LOD, gradients, offsets, projected coordinates, gathers, compares, resource queries, multisample query/fetch forms, resource arrays, and deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid advanced combinations.

features.description: Readable and writable image resources with explicit formats and access qualifiers.
features.name: Storage image load/store

directx.notes: Covers typed RWTexture load/store for scalar, vector, narrow, explicit-format, array, access-qualified, and rasterizer-ordered images, with deterministic diagnostics for incompatible access.

metal.notes: Covers texture read/write for scalar, vector, narrow, explicit-format, array, and access-qualified images, with deterministic diagnostics for incompatible access.

opengl.notes: Covers image load/store for scalar, vector, narrow, explicit-format, array, and access-qualified images, with deterministic diagnostics for incompatible access.

features.description: Atomic operations on image resources.
features.name: Image atomics

directx.notes: Covers integer image atomic add/min/max/bitwise/exchange/compare-exchange across supported dimensions, including rasterizer-ordered textures, and emits deterministic diagnostics for unsupported formats and multisample atomics.

metal.notes: Covers integer texture atomics add/min/max/bitwise/exchange/compare-exchange across supported dimensions and emits deterministic diagnostics for unsupported formats and multisample atomics.

opengl.notes: Covers integer image atomic add/min/max/bitwise/exchange/compare-exchange across supported dimensions and emits deterministic diagnostics for unsupported formats and argument shapes.

features.description: Represent multisample storage images and report unsupported load/store/atomic combinations deterministically.
features.name: Multisample storage images

directx.notes: Represents multisample storage images as read-capable SRVs, preserves size/sample queries and arrays, and emits deterministic diagnostics for DirectX-unsupported stores and atomics.

metal.notes: Represents multisample storage images as read-capable textures, preserves size/sample queries and arrays, and emits deterministic diagnostics for Metal-unsupported stores and atomics.

opengl.notes: Covers multisample image load/store, sample-count and size queries, arrays, typed formats, and integer atomics where GLSL permits them.

features.description: Lower struct declarations, member access, initialization, and return values.
features.name: Struct declarations and construction

features.description: Lower fixed arrays, array parameters, indexing, and array return diagnostics.
features.name: Array declarations and access

features.description: Lower helper functions, entry functions, call expressions, and return statements.
features.name: Function declarations and calls

features.description: if/else, loops, switch, break, continue, and return statements.
features.name: Control flow

features.description: Lower barrier, memoryBarrier, and workgroupBarrier calls to backend-native synchronization intrinsics.
features.name: Synchronization and memory barriers

directx.notes: barrier/workgroupBarrier lower to GroupMemoryBarrierWithGroupSync; memoryBarrier lowers to AllMemoryBarrier; user-defined functions with the same names are preserved.

metal.notes: barrier/workgroupBarrier, groupMemoryBarrier, and memoryBarrierShared lower to threadgroup barriers; memoryBarrier, memoryBarrierBuffer, and deviceMemoryBarrier lower to device-memory barriers; memoryBarrierImage lowers to a texture-memory barrier; allMemoryBarrier lowers to combined device/threadgroup/texture flags; threadgroup atomic arrays and GLSL buffer-block atomic compare helpers use address-form atomic operations across helper/kernel barrier ordering; atomic compare-exchange lowers target and expected-value address forms to toolchain-compatible Metal intrinsics, with deterministic diagnostics for unsupported expected-pointer address spaces; user-defined functions with the same names are preserved.

opengl.notes: workgroupBarrier lowers to GLSL barrier; barrier and memoryBarrier remain native; user-defined workgroupBarrier functions are preserved.

features.description: Lower CrossGL Wave* subgroup operations including lane queries, reductions, scans, votes, ballots, and lane reads.
features.name: Wave/subgroup intrinsics

directx.notes: WaveOpNode calls are preserved as native HLSL Wave* intrinsics across lane queries, votes, reductions, scans, ballots, WaveMatch, WaveMultiPrefix*, quad reads, and arity/type diagnostics. Optional DXC validation covers a compute wave shader when dxc is installed.

metal.notes: Lowers CrossGL Wave* operations to Metal simdgroup intrinsics or helper functions, threads compute-stage lane metadata through helper calls, and emits compile-safe diagnostics for contexts where Metal cannot supply simdgroup lane values.

opengl.notes: Lowers CrossGL Wave* lane, vote, reduction, ballot, shuffle, quad, WaveMatch, and WaveMultiPrefix* operations to GL_KHR_shader_subgroup builtins or local helper functions with required extension declarations, deterministic arity/type diagnostics, and glslang validation coverage.

features.description: CrossGL match expressions and pattern arms.
features.name: Match/pattern lowering

directx.notes: Covers switch-lowerable literal/wildcard arms, guarded if-chain arms, identifier bindings, struct fields, enum path and payload patterns, generic enum matches, match expressions, scoped case bodies, and shader validation with texture/image resources.

metal.notes: Covers switch-lowerable literal/wildcard arms, guarded if-chain arms, identifier bindings, struct fields, enum path and payload patterns, generic enum matches, match expressions, scoped case bodies, and shader validation with texture/image resources.

opengl.notes: Covers switch-lowerable literal/wildcard arms, guarded if-chain arms, identifier bindings, struct fields, enum path and payload patterns, generic enum matches, match expressions, scoped case bodies, and shader validation with texture/image resources.

features.description: Vector/matrix constructors, arithmetic, swizzles, casts, and helper intrinsics.
features.name: Vector and matrix expressions

features.description: Integer bitwise operators and assignment variants.
features.name: Bitwise operations

features.description: Backend-specific validation for invalid semantics, stages, resource shapes, and builtin types.
features.name: Invalid shader shape validation

metal.notes: Metal codegen validates geometry maxvertexcount/input primitive shapes, tessellation patch metadata and domain-location shapes, and invalid texture, image, resource-array, and resource-query shapes with deterministic errors or compile-safe diagnostics.

features.description: Discover registered shader and GPU source files across a repository without assuming a single source backend.
features.name: Repository source discovery

directx.notes: The project scanner discovers registered shader/GPU source files across configured source roots, includes configured and CLI source override patterns during default discovery, records unsupported explicit includes, reports invalid roots and non-repository-relative scan patterns without hiding valid units, rejects malformed project configuration scalar and source override metadata before scan, and makes scan/report CLI commands exit nonzero when the generated report contains error diagnostics.

metal.notes: The project scanner discovers registered shader/GPU source files across configured source roots, includes configured and CLI source override patterns during default discovery, records unsupported explicit includes, reports invalid roots and non-repository-relative scan patterns without hiding valid units, rejects malformed project configuration scalar and source override metadata before scan, and makes scan/report CLI commands exit nonzero when the generated report contains error diagnostics.

opengl.notes: The project scanner discovers registered shader/GPU source files across configured source roots, includes configured and CLI source override patterns during default discovery, records unsupported explicit includes, reports invalid roots and non-repository-relative scan patterns without hiding valid units, rejects malformed project configuration scalar and source override metadata before scan, and makes scan/report CLI commands exit nonzero when the generated report contains error diagnostics.

features.description: Carry project include-directory intent into repo-scale translation planning.
features.name: Project include resolution

directx.notes: The project pipeline records include directories, reports missing include directories, reports include directories that resolve outside the repository, applies CLI include-directory overrides, and passes resolved paths to source frontends that expose include-path preprocessor options. Include semantics remain backend-dependent, so this remains partial project support.

metal.notes: The project pipeline records include directories, reports missing include directories, reports include directories that resolve outside the repository, applies CLI include-directory overrides, and passes resolved paths to source frontends that expose include-path preprocessor options. Include semantics remain backend-dependent, so this remains partial project support.

opengl.notes: The project pipeline records include directories, reports missing include directories, reports include directories that resolve outside the repository, applies CLI include-directory overrides, and passes resolved paths to source frontends that expose include-path preprocessor options. Include semantics remain backend-dependent, so this remains partial project support.

features.description: Represent define sets and expand named variants for project translation units.
features.name: Macro and define variants

directx.notes: The project pipeline records define sets and named variant metadata in portability reports, preserves define and variant maps alongside counts, validates malformed define/variant metadata and undeclared artifact variants in reports, rejects malformed define and variant TOML values before scan, applies CLI define overrides, passes defines to source frontends that expose preprocessor options, and expands configured named variants into separate artifact attempts with base defines merged with variant overrides. Native preprocessor semantics remain backend-dependent, so this remains partial project support.

metal.notes: The project pipeline records define sets and named variant metadata in portability reports, preserves define and variant maps alongside counts, validates malformed define/variant metadata and undeclared artifact variants in reports, rejects malformed define and variant TOML values before scan, applies CLI define overrides, passes defines to source frontends that expose preprocessor options, and expands configured named variants into separate artifact attempts with base defines merged with variant overrides. Native preprocessor semantics remain backend-dependent, so this remains partial project support.

opengl.notes: The project pipeline records define sets and named variant metadata in portability reports, preserves define and variant maps alongside counts, validates malformed define/variant metadata and undeclared artifact variants in reports, rejects malformed define and variant TOML values before scan, applies CLI define overrides, passes defines to source frontends that expose preprocessor options, and expands configured named variants into separate artifact attempts with base defines merged with variant overrides. Native preprocessor semantics remain backend-dependent, so this remains partial project support.

features.description: Translate discovered project units to one or more target backends while preserving repository-relative output paths.
features.name: Batch project translation

directx.notes: The project pipeline batches through the existing single-file translator, normalizes duplicate explicit targets, applies source backend overrides, records unsupported target diagnostics, preserves relative output paths, refuses to write artifacts outside the repository, and records failures per artifact. Full multi-corpus target coverage remains ongoing.

metal.notes: The project pipeline batches through the existing single-file translator, normalizes duplicate explicit targets, applies source backend overrides, records unsupported target diagnostics, preserves relative output paths, refuses to write artifacts outside the repository, and records failures per artifact. Full multi-corpus target coverage remains ongoing.

features.description: Validate report metadata, artifact records, failed artifacts, translated artifact existence, optional external toolchain availability, and opt-in toolchain smoke failures for project outputs.
features.name: Optional validation hooks

directx.notes: Project validation checks report contract shape, rejects malformed generated timestamp and generator metadata, project/config metadata, unit and skipped records, artifact records including duplicate artifact identities and required current translated artifact hashes, source-map records and anchor consistency, external corpus records and summaries, summary rollups, migration actions and kinds, and preserved diagnostics, accepts reports that skip malformed external corpus manifest entries with warnings, rejects artifacts and validation records for targets not declared by the report, rejects artifacts for variants not declared by the report, records failed artifacts, checks translated artifact existence, skips missing or escaped artifacts before opt-in toolchain smoke checks, reports unavailable optional tools deterministically, validates validation result, validation artifact hash status fields, aggregate validation summaries, and toolchain run records, exposes inspect-report JSON/text summaries with validation-failed artifact lists, diagnostic-code, missing-capability, validation hash-status, and external-corpus rollups plus nonzero status for validation errors, and turns opt-in toolchain smoke failures into structured diagnostics. Full target compiler invocation remains opt-in and is not required for basic translation.

metal.notes: Project validation checks report contract shape, rejects malformed generated timestamp and generator metadata, project/config metadata, unit and skipped records, artifact records including duplicate artifact identities and required current translated artifact hashes, source-map records and anchor consistency, external corpus records and summaries, summary rollups, migration actions and kinds, and preserved diagnostics, accepts reports that skip malformed external corpus manifest entries with warnings, rejects artifacts and validation records for targets not declared by the report, rejects artifacts for variants not declared by the report, records failed artifacts, checks translated artifact existence, skips missing or escaped artifacts before opt-in toolchain smoke checks, reports unavailable optional tools deterministically, validates validation result, validation artifact hash status fields, aggregate validation summaries, and toolchain run records, exposes inspect-report JSON/text summaries with validation-failed artifact lists, diagnostic-code, missing-capability, validation hash-status, and external-corpus rollups plus nonzero status for validation errors, and turns opt-in toolchain smoke failures into structured diagnostics. features.description: Emit structured manual follow-up actions for repo-porting work outside shader and kernel source translation.
features.name: Migration action report

directx.notes: Project reports emit documented manual-runtime-integration actions when supported translation units and target backends are present, omit unsupported scan targets, scope translation-report actions to targets with translated artifacts, and validation rejects action targets that are not declared by the report. The action message separates shader/kernel source translation from host runtime APIs, resource binding setup, build scripts, and backend framework integration.

metal.notes: Project reports emit documented manual-runtime-integration actions when supported translation units and target backends are present, omit unsupported scan targets, scope translation-report actions to targets with translated artifacts, and validation rejects action targets that are not declared by the report. The action message separates shader/kernel source translation from host runtime APIs, resource binding setup, build scripts, and backend framework integration.

opengl.notes: Project reports emit documented manual-runtime-integration actions when supported translation units and target backends are present, omit unsupported scan targets, scope translation-report actions to targets with translated artifacts, and validation rejects action targets that are not declared by the report. The action message separates shader/kernel source translation from host runtime APIs, resource binding setup, build scripts, and backend framework integration.

features.description: Emit machine-readable translated artifact records with source, target, path, status, source hash, and generated artifact hash metadata.
features.name: Artifact manifest

directx.notes: The portability report records report generation time and generator package version, and records every attempted artifact with source backend, target backend, output path, translation status, source hash, generated artifact hash, basic pipeline provenance, and failed records for artifact writes blocked by invalid project output paths. Validation rejects duplicate artifact identities, artifact records whose targets are not declared by the report, current translated artifacts without source or generated hashes, malformed source backend, source hash, generated hash, or provenance metadata, source artifacts whose current file hash no longer matches the report, missing source artifacts with recorded hashes, and generated artifacts whose current file hash no longer matches the report. Validation artifact checks also expose source and generated hash status fields plus aggregate summary counts.

metal.notes: The portability report records report generation time and generator package version, and records every attempted artifact with source backend, target backend, output path, translation status, source hash, generated artifact hash, basic pipeline provenance, and failed records for artifact writes blocked by invalid project output paths. Validation rejects duplicate artifact identities, artifact records whose targets are not declared by the report, current translated artifacts without source or generated hashes, malformed source backend, source hash, generated hash, or provenance metadata, source artifacts whose current file hash no longer matches the report, missing source artifacts with recorded hashes, and generated artifacts whose current file hash no longer matches the report. Validation artifact checks also expose source and generated hash status fields plus aggregate summary counts.

opengl.notes: The portability report records report generation time and generator package version, and records every attempted artifact with source backend, target backend, output path, translation status, source hash, generated artifact hash, basic pipeline provenance, and failed records for artifact writes blocked by invalid project output paths. Validation rejects duplicate artifact identities, artifact records whose targets are not declared by the report, current translated artifacts without source or generated hashes, malformed source backend, source hash, generated hash, or provenance metadata, source artifacts whose current file hash no longer matches the report, missing source artifacts with recorded hashes, and generated artifacts whose current file hash no longer matches the report. Validation artifact checks also expose source and generated hash status fields plus aggregate summary counts.

features.description: Record provenance metadata that can bridge toward compiler source-map and debug metadata contracts.
features.name: Source provenance and source-map bridge

directx.notes: Project reports include configured source override maps, source hashes, relative paths, source backend names, intermediate CrossGL provenance, and file-granularity artifact source-map anchors. Report validation accepts generated source maps, records per-artifact source hash status and aggregate hash-status counts, and rejects malformed project source override metadata, source-map records, inconsistent source-map anchors, source hash records, source artifacts whose current file hash does not match the report, missing source artifacts with recorded hashes, and provenance records before artifact validation. Fine-grained source maps are not implemented in the translator project pipeline yet.

metal.notes: Project reports include configured source override maps, source hashes, relative paths, source backend names, intermediate CrossGL provenance, and file-granularity artifact source-map anchors. Report validation accepts generated source maps, records per-artifact source hash status and aggregate hash-status counts, and rejects malformed project source override metadata, source-map records, inconsistent source-map anchors, source hash records, source artifacts whose current file hash does not match the report, missing source artifacts with recorded hashes, and provenance records before artifact validation. Fine-grained source maps are not implemented in the translator project pipeline yet.

opengl.notes: Project reports include configured source override maps, source hashes, relative paths, source backend names, intermediate CrossGL provenance, and file-granularity artifact source-map anchors. Report validation accepts generated source maps, records per-artifact source hash status and aggregate hash-status counts, and rejects malformed project source override metadata, source-map records, inconsistent source-map anchors, source hash records, source artifacts whose current file hash does not match the report, missing source artifacts with recorded hashes, and provenance records before artifact validation. Fine-grained source maps are not implemented in the translator project pipeline yet.

features.description: Emit structured project diagnostics for scan, translation, and validation failures.
features.name: Project diagnostics

directx.notes: Project diagnostics use compiler-compatible severity, code, message, location, target, and missing-capability fields while keeping per-artifact failures in the report. Scan/report CLI commands return nonzero when project reports contain error diagnostics, unsupported target names and non-repository-relative scan patterns are reported before validation, and validation rejects malformed preserved diagnostics before copying them into validation output. Report summaries include contract-checked rollups by diagnostic code and missing capability.

metal.notes: Project diagnostics use compiler-compatible severity, code, message, location, target, and missing-capability fields while keeping per-artifact failures in the report. Scan/report CLI commands return nonzero when project reports contain error diagnostics, unsupported target names and non-repository-relative scan patterns are reported before validation, and validation rejects malformed preserved diagnostics before copying them into validation output. Report summaries include contract-checked rollups by diagnostic code and missing capability.

opengl.notes: Project diagnostics use compiler-compatible severity, code, message, location, target, and missing-capability fields while keeping per-artifact failures in the report. Scan/report CLI commands return nonzero when project reports contain error diagnostics, unsupported target names and non-repository-relative scan patterns are reported before validation, and validation rejects malformed preserved diagnostics before copying them into validation output. Report summaries include contract-checked rollups by diagnostic code and missing capability.

features.description: Track repository-scale coverage against external shader and GPU source corpora.
features.name: External corpus coverage

directx.notes: Project reports can attach a repository-relative external corpus manifest, skip malformed manifest entries with structured diagnostics, and summarize declared valid paths, present and missing entries, discovered translation units, source-backend, target, and artifact rollups for manifest-backed project runs, and text inspection surfaces the same rollups. The pinned reduced-corpus manifest includes a focused DirectX reduction covered by backend fixture tests. This is a coverage and accounting layer only: CrossTL does not clone upstream repositories, run native build systems, or claim whole-corpus semantic parity from reduced backend fixtures.

metal.notes: Project reports can attach a repository-relative external corpus manifest, skip malformed manifest entries with structured diagnostics, and summarize declared valid paths, present and missing entries, discovered translation units, source-backend, target, and artifact rollups for manifest-backed project runs, and text inspection surfaces the same rollups. The pinned reduced-corpus manifest includes a focused Metal reduction covered by backend fixture tests. This is a coverage and accounting layer only: CrossTL does not clone upstream repositories, run native build systems, or claim whole-corpus semantic parity from reduced backend fixtures.

opengl.notes: Project reports can attach a repository-relative external corpus manifest, skip malformed manifest entries with structured diagnostics, and summarize declared valid paths, present and missing entries, discovered translation units, source-backend, target, and artifact rollups for manifest-backed project runs, and text inspection surfaces the same rollups. The pinned reduced-corpus manifest includes a focused OpenGL reduction covered by backend fixture tests. This is a coverage and accounting layer only: CrossTL does not clone upstream repositories, run native build systems, or claim whole-corpus semantic parity from reduced backend fixtures.
